translation: Alif, Lam, Meem
commentary:
الٓمٓ

Alif Lam Meem. These are the disjointed letters that open this beautiful surah, one of the favorite chapters of the Prophet ﷺ. He used to prefer reciting this surah in the first rakah of Fajr on Friday, and it's highly recommended for us to memorize it. At only three to four pages, it's relatively short and accessible for memorization, making it perfect for following the Sunnah of the Messenger ﷺ.

translation: [This is] the revelation of the Book about which there is no doubt from the Lord of the worlds
commentary:
تَنزِيلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ

The revelation of the Book in which there is absolutely no doubt whatsoever - it is from the Master of all nations and all peoples. Allah ﷻ immediately establishes the divine origin of the Quran. This is تَنزِيل - the act of revelation coming down from the highest authority. There is لَا رَيْبَ - no doubt, no suspicion, no room for questioning its authenticity. And it comes مِن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ - from the Master, the Sustainer, the Lord of all worlds, all peoples, all nations.

translation: Or do they say, "He invented it"? Rather, it is the truth from your Lord, [O Muhammad], that you may warn a people to whom no warner has come before you [so] perhaps they will be guided
commentary:
أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۚ بَلْ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ لِتُنذِرَ قَوْمًا مَّآ أَتَىٰهُم مِّن نَّذِيرٍ مِّن قَبْلِكَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ

Is it the case then that they're saying he's made it up? Rather, the fact is, it's the truth from your Master so that you can warn a nation to whom no warner had come for a long time before you, so that they themselves can become committed to guidance.

The disbelievers accuse the Prophet ﷺ of fabricating the Quran, but Allah ﷻ dismisses this immediately. بَلْ - rather, on the contrary - it is الْحَقُّ, the absolute truth from your Master. The Quraysh and the specific region of Makkah hadn't received a messenger since the time of Ibrahim and Ismail (AS). Neighboring nations like the people of Madyan, Thamud and others had received messengers, but this particular region had been without divine guidance for centuries. 

The Prophet ﷺ was sent لِتُنذِرَ - so that you can warn them. Warning implies both danger ahead and the compassion to alert someone about it. And the purpose? لَعَلَّهُمْ يَهْتَدُونَ - so that they themselves can become committed to guidance, so that they can find their way.

translation: It is Allah who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days; then He established Himself above the Throne. You have not besides Him any protector or any intercessor; so will you not be reminded
commentary:
ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۖ مَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا شَفِيعٍ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ

Allah is the One Who created the heavens and the earth and whatever lies between the two of them in six days, then He rose and balanced Himself upon the Throne. You do not have any protective friend other than Him, nor any intercessor. Why then do you not take advice?

Allah ﷻ is الَّذِى خَلَقَ - the One Who created. Everything we see - the skies, the earth, and everything between them - came into existence through His creative power فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ, in six days. But we must understand something critical here: our concept of a "day" is based on the positioning of the sun. We organize time around the cycle of the sun rising and setting. But when Allah ﷻ says He created everything in six days, part of that creation process was the sun itself. This means the way He describes "day" cannot be the same as our 24-hour cycle. His concept of time is fundamentally different from our own - something this surah will continue to elaborate on.

ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ - then He rose and established Himself upon the Throne. This is an expression of His ultimate authority and kingship. The Throne represents the seat of power, of command, of sovereignty. When a king takes his position on the throne, it signifies the establishment of his rule and authority.

And if He is established as the ultimate King, then مَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِهِۦ مِن وَلِىٍّ - you're not going to find any protective friend other than Him. No one else can shield you, protect you, or take care of your affairs. وَلَا شَفِيعٍ - and nor will you find anybody to come and stand before Him and make a case on your behalf, to intercede for you.

أَفَلَا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ - Why then do you not take advice? Why don't you make an effort to remember?

translation: He arranges [each] matter from the heaven to the earth; then it will ascend to Him in a Day, the extent of which is a thousand years of those which you count
commentary:
يُدَبِّرُ ٱلْأَمْرَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ يَعْرُجُ إِلَيْهِ فِى يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُۥٓ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ

He plans out the decision from the heaven to the earth, then it ascends to Him in a day, the measure of which is a thousand years of what you count.

يُدَبِّرُ - He plans out in intricate detail. تَدْبِير means to plan something from A to Z, to think through the first phase and all the phases that come after. The word دُبُر literally means "back" - what comes after, what follows. So تَدْبِير is planning not just the immediate action but everything that comes after it, after it, after it - a complete, comprehensive plan.

He plans out الْأَمْرَ - the decision, the command, the matter. What decision? What to do with every single creature on earth. What to do today, what to do tomorrow, what to do in the next minute. All those decisions are made مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ. This could mean the entire range - from the highest heavens all the way down to the earth, He's planning decisions. It could also mean that from way above, from His Throne, He makes these decisions and then they're delivered down to the earth, carried out in our realm.

ثُمَّ يَعْرُجُ إِلَيْهِ - then these decisions, their implications, their execution, and the report ascends back to Him. فِى يَوْمٍ كَانَ مِقْدَارُهُۥٓ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ - and that one day, its measure is a thousand years from what you people count. 

Notice the powerful expression: مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ - from what you can count. It's not even exactly a thousand years - it's a thousand years from within what you might be able to count. Allah ﷻ is telling us: a day with Me is not like a day that you have. If you want some idea from within your framework of understanding, from within what you're capable of counting, think of it as a thousand years. But even that doesn't capture it fully.

This connects to a fundamental understanding of creation. There are two worlds Allah ﷻ has created: the world of خَلْق - physical creation - and the world of أَمْر - command, the unseen realm. The seen world includes the universe, the solar systems, the planets, the galaxies. The unseen world includes the angels, the ruh (soul), Paradise and Hellfire, the jinn. 

The world of khalq has a certain quality - it goes through processes. A tree doesn't just instantly appear; it starts as a seed, it grows over time, and eventually becomes a tree. Human beings aren't born educated; they learn and develop over years. Everything in the physical world is subject to time and process.

But the world of amr is instantaneous. An angel can travel from the seventh heaven to earth in an instant. If we tried to reach even the sun, or another planet or solar system with our best technology, it would take us an infinite amount of time. The world of amr isn't limited by the time constraints that the world of khalq is subject to. They're two different dimensions.

The human being is unique - we're a combination of both. Our body is from khalq, but the ruh is from amr. يَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلرُّوحِ ۖ قُلِ ٱلرُّوحُ مِنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّى - "They ask you about the soul. Say: The soul is from the command of my Master." (17:85) That's why at night, the ruh travels - it can be taken from the body and then delivered back. The body can't make that journey, but the ruh can. There's a fundamental difference between these two dimensions of creation.

All of khalq - physical creation - is under, dictated by, and subject to amr. Sometimes amr interferes with khalq. Fire burns - that's the nature of khalq. But sometimes the amr of Allah comes and says, "No, today fire won't burn" - like with Ibrahim (AS). Or water normally retains its shape based on its container, but when the amr of Allah strikes Musa's (AS) staff against it, the water parts. In modern terms, we'd say the laws of nature were broken. But really, it's the world of amr imposing itself on the world of khalq.

The Quran is from the amr of Allah - it's revelation from the unseen realm. That's why it's not subject to any change, any alteration. It can't be modified or destroyed, because all attempts to change it would be attempts from khalq, and the Quran is beyond that.

Even the fitrah of the human being - the natural disposition that every newborn has, which is also called the ruh, which is the amr of Allah - that's why every single newborn has it. It doesn't matter when, where, or how they were born, what ethnicity, what century, whether they have physical impairments or not. Those are concerns of the body, problems of khalq. But the ruh will always be there. That guarantee is there because it's from amr.

When Allah ﷻ says يُدَبِّرُ ٱلْأَمْرَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ - He sends His amr from the sky to the earth - these decisions from the realm of amr have their own time definitions. The deeds we do in khalq are taken back to Him, and they have their own time framework. But if you try to compare it to time in khalq, we can only measure it as something like a thousand years - and even that's not exact, because Allah ﷻ added مِّمَّا تَعُدُّونَ - from what you, in your limited, relative sense, can count. It's not an actual equation - it's beyond our full comprehension.

translation: That is the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Exalted in Might, the Merciful
commentary:
ذَٰلِكَ عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ

That is the Knower of the unseen and the seen, the Almighty, the Merciful.

After describing the world of amr and khalq, Allah ﷻ declares: ذَٰلِكَ - That One, the One we've been describing, is عَـٰلِمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ -the complete Knower of both the unseen and the seen. الْغَيْب is the world of amr - everything hidden from our perception. الشَّهَـٰدَة is the world of khalq - everything we can witness and observe.

He is ٱلْعَزِيزُ - the Almighty, the One of ultimate authority, the One Whose power cannot be challenged or overcome. And He is ٱلرَّحِيمُ - the always Merciful, the One Whose mercy encompasses all of His creation. These two names together show us that while He has absolute power and authority over both realms, His exercise of that power is always tempered with mercy.

translation: Who perfected everything which He created and began the creation of man from clay
commentary:
ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْسَنَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقَهُۥ ۖ وَبَدَأَ خَلْقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنِ مِن طِينٍ

The One Who perfected everything He created, and He began the creation of the human being from clay.

Allah ﷻ just spoke about His amr - His command, the unseen realm. Now He turns our attention to His khalq - His physical creation. ٱلَّذِىٓ أَحْسَنَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقَهُۥ - the One Who excelled in everything, who made beautiful everything, who perfected everything in terms of its creation.

Whatever He created, He excelled in its design, its function, its purpose. Everything He made is أَحْسَن - the best it could possibly be, perfectly suited for what it was created to do.

وَبَدَأَ خَلْقَ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنِ مِن طِينٍ - And then He initiated, He started the creation of the human being مِن طِينٍ - from clay, from wet soil. This is referring to the original creation of Adam (AS), our father, who was fashioned from the earth itself.

translation: Then He made his posterity out of the extract of a liquid disdained
commentary:
ثُمَّ جَعَلَ نَسْلَهُۥ مِن سُلَـٰلَةٍ مِّن مَّآءٍ مَّهِينٍ

Then He made his offspring from an extract of a humiliating fluid.

After creating Adam (AS) from clay, Allah ﷻ established a different process for his descendants. ثُمَّ جَعَلَ نَسْلَهُۥ - then He made his running offspring. The word نَسْل refers to descendants, but it comes from the root meaning to run down, like running down a hill from a height.

مِن سُلَـٰلَةٍ - from an extract, from the essence of something. سُلَالَة is like when you draw a sword from its sheath - it's the pure essence that's extracted. Or like squeezing juice from a fruit - the concentrated essence. مِّن مَّآءٍ مَّهِينٍ - from a fluid that is weak, humble, insignificant, and prone to destruction.

SubhanAllah, even modern biology teaches us that as sperm travels, millions are killed along the way. They're weak, vulnerable, under attack. Only one might survive to reach its destination and create new life. Allah ﷻ calls this مَاءٍ مَّهِينٍ - a weak, humiliated form of water, susceptible to danger, something that is attacked and destroyed. This is the humble beginning of every human being after Adam (AS).

translation: Then He proportioned him and breathed into him from His [created] soul and made for you hearing and vision and hearts; little are you grateful
commentary:
ثُمَّ سَوَّىٰهُ وَنَفَخَ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِهِۦ ۖ وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ ۚ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ

Then He proportioned him and breathed into him from His spirit, and He made for you hearing, sight and hearts. Little thanks do you show!

ثُمَّ سَوَّىٰهُ - Then He proportioned him, fashioned him, balanced him out. The word تَسْوِيَة means to balance something, to make it proportional and harmonious. Think of a child playing with clay or Play-Doh - "I'm going to make this arm this way, I'll shape that part like this, I'll build the features just so." This is the careful, deliberate fashioning that Allah ﷻ does with every human being in the womb.

The mother's body becomes the place where Allah ﷻ molds and shapes the child. Every feature, every organ, every cell is carefully balanced and proportioned according to His perfect design.

وَنَفَخَ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِهِۦ - and then He blew into it from His special spirit. This is the critical moment - when the pregnancy reaches a certain stage, around 120 days, an angel comes with the amr of Allah and breathes the ruh into the developing child.

This is transformative. Up until this point, all of the human being's creation up to 120 days is khalq - physical development, cellular growth, organic formation. Then suddenly, the angel comes and adds the amr - the ruh, the soul - and the human being becomes a unique combination of both dimensions. We're made up of body and soul, khalq and amr, the physical and the spiritual.

Some poets and philosophers used to describe human nature as part angel and part animal. There's something to this insight. The ruh is a creature of light - it has lofty, noble qualities. It seeks the divine, it yearns for goodness, it's capable of rising to incredible spiritual heights. But we also have the physical body with its appetites, just like animals have. We have desires for food, comfort, security. We can be defensive, protective, aggressive when threatened - just like animals.

We're this strange, unique mix of these two realities. And actually, we're the only creature in the entire universe that finds this perfect balance between the physical and the spiritual, between body and soul. The angels are pure spirit with no physical desires. Animals are pure body with no spiritual awareness. But we humans? We're both, and that's what makes us so special and so tested.

وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَٱلْأَفْـِٔدَةَ - and He made for you the hearing, the sights, and the hearts. Notice the order: hearing comes first, then sight, then the heart. We receive information through our ears and eyes, and that information settles in our hearts where we process it, understand it, and make decisions based on it.

قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ - How little thanks you show! After all this miraculous creation, this careful design, this perfect combination of body and soul, this gift of faculties to perceive and understand - how little gratitude we actually express.

translation: And they say, "When we are lost within the earth, will we indeed be [recreated] in a new creation?" Rather, they are, in [the matter of] the meeting with their Lord, disbelievers
commentary:
وَقَالُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا ضَلَلْنَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَءِنَّا لَفِى خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍۭ ۚ بَلْ هُم بِلِقَآءِ رَبِّهِمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ

And they said, "When we are lost in the earth, will we really be in a new creation?" Rather, they are in denial of the meeting with their Master.

The disbelievers respond to all this talk of resurrection with mockery: وَقَالُوٓا۟ أَءِذَا ضَلَلْنَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - "When we get lost in the earth?" The word ضَلَلْنَا means to be lost, to wander, to disappear without a trace. They're saying: "When we're buried deep in the ground, when our graves don't even have markings anymore, when people walk over us without knowing we're there, when we're completely decomposed and scattered in the earth..."

أَءِنَّا لَفِى خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ - "Are we really going to be brought back in a completely new creation?" They find it absurd, impossible to believe.

But Allah ﷻ cuts through their mockery with the truth: بَلْ هُم بِلِقَآءِ رَبِّهِمْ كَـٰفِرُونَ - Rather, the reality is that in meeting with their Master, in the very concept of meeting with their Master, they are in complete denial. They're kaafiroon - they reject it, cover it up, refuse to accept it.

This passage is honoring the creation process, showing how much detail, how much planning, how much precision went into creating human beings. The point is clear: How can you think that Allah ﷻ created something with such intricate planning and design, yet it's here for no purpose? That if it didn't meet its purpose, it won't have to answer for why it was created? That we were just created as play, for no reason, with no accountability?

This is actually taught in some philosophy courses - the idea that perhaps God created this world out of boredom, that He's just playing with us. This is ultimate blasphemy. These are the people in denial - they convince themselves we're just evolved creatures, that organic matter accidentally appeared on this planet after some random explosion, and here we are.

But think about your own experience: What in your life suggests that random explosions create perfect order? That chaos produces precision? That accidents result in beauty and purpose? You have to really go out of your way to disbelieve. You have to actively choose to ignore what's right in front of you.

translation: Say, "The angel of death will take you who has been entrusted with you. Then to your Lord you will be returned
commentary:
۞ قُلْ يَتَوَفَّىٰكُم مَّلَكُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ٱلَّذِى وُكِّلَ بِكُمْ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ

Say: "The Angel of Death who has been put in charge of you will take you, then to your Master you will be returned."

قُلْ - Tell them, O Prophet يَتَوَفَّىٰكُم مَّلَكُ ٱلْمَوْتِ  - The Malak of death will take you away. The word مَلَك means angel, but مَلِك means king. There's a beautiful wordplay here - the Angel of Death is also called the King of Death in some readings, because he has authority over this domain, this transition from life to death.

ٱلَّذِى وُُكِّلَ بِكُمْ - the one who has been put in charge of your matter, who has been entrusted with this job. When Allah ﷻ commands him to take a particular soul, he goes and takes it. No negotiations, no delays, no exceptions.

Imagine the scene: A person is young, healthy, with their whole life ahead of them. Suddenly the Angel of Death appears. "Wait, no, not yet! I'm so young! Can't you at least put in a request for a few more weeks? Let me make some last tawbah!" But that's not how this works. There's no negotiation. The appointed time has come, and it's done.

A close friend who works in the ICU at a hospital sees death regularly. He says, "I swear I can tell when a person is about to go. They just look up, and the shock on their face - I can see it. They're seeing the Angel of Death. And I sit there thinking: this Angel of Death is in the room with me right now. He's looking at me, thinking, 'Yeah, not you yet. You've got more time. But your turn is coming - I've got you on the schedule.' And then he passes right by me and takes that person."

ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ - Then to your Master, all of you will be returned. This is the ultimate destination. After the Angel of Death takes your soul, you're going back to the One Who created you, the One Who gave you life, the One to Whom you belong.

translation: If you could but see when the criminals are hanging their heads before their Lord, [saying], "Our Lord, we have seen and heard, so return us [to the world]; we will work righteousness. Indeed, we are [now] certain
commentary:
وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ إِذِ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ نَاكِسُوا۟ رُءُوسِهِمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ رَبَّنَآ أَبْصَرْنَا وَسَمِعْنَا فَٱرْجِعْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا إِنَّا مُوقِنُونَ

If only you could see when the criminals will be holding their heads down before their Master: "Our Master! We have seen and we have heard, so send us back and we will act righteously. Indeed, we are now certain!"

وَلَوْ تَرَىٰٓ - If you could only see, if you had been able to witness this scene. إِذِ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ نَاكِسُوا۟ رُءُوسِهِمْ - when the criminals are holding their heads down, bowed in shame and humiliation.

نَاكِس means to lower one's head, to be brought low. It's like when a nation's flag is lowered - it's a sign of defeat, of humiliation. نَاكِسُوا۟ رُءُوسِهِمْ - their heads are permanently lowered. They can't even lift them for one second. They've become people who can only look down.

And here's the irony: when you put your head down, you can't see properly. Your field of vision is cut off. But on Judgment Day, the person with their head down says, "I don't need to see anymore. I've seen enough. I don't even dare look up."

عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ - before their Master, in His presence. And what do they say? رَبَّنَآ أَبْصَرْنَا وَسَمِعْنَا - "Our Master, please! We've seen enough! We've heard now!"

Notice something crucial here: they mention seeing before hearing. Typically in the Quran, Allah ﷻ mentions hearing first and sight second - السَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ. Why? Because the message of Islam is first and foremost meant to be heard. The Prophet ﷺ didn't come to show people miracles first - he came to speak to them, to recite to them, to convey the message. Musa (AS) didn't come to show Fir'awn something - he came to talk to him. When all else fails, then the miracles are shown. So hearing comes first, and seeing comes second.

But on Judgment Day, for these people who used to say "We won't believe until we see something," Allah ﷻ reverses the order. You wanted to see? Fine - there's plenty for you to see on Judgment Day. Now that you've seen, are you ready to listen?

فَٱرْجِعْنَا نَعْمَلْ صَـٰلِحًا - "Send us back! We'll act differently! We'll be different people!" إِنَّا مُوقِنُونَ - "We are absolutely, completely convinced now!"

يَقِين in Arabic means to believe something with the certainty that comes from seeing it with your own eyes. It's not just intellectual belief - it's conviction born from direct experience. Like when you tell someone there's a mouse in the house. "Really? Did you hear it?" "No, I SAW it. It was looking right at me." That's yaqeen - you're absolutely certain because you witnessed it yourself.

These people have yaqeen on Judgment Day because they're experiencing it. But in the previous surah, what did Allah ﷻ say about the people of the Quran? ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ لَا رَيْبَ ۛ فِيهِ ۛ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ (2:2,3) - This is the Book in which there is no doubt, a guidance for the righteous, those who believe in the unseen. And then: وَبِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِهُمْ يُوقِنُونَ (2:4) - and they have complete conviction in the Hereafter.

They have yaqeen about the Akhirah even though they can't see it yet. What gives them that? Revelation gives them that. The Quran gives them such a vivid, powerful picture of the Akhirah that it becomes real to them. It's not a theory - it's a reality they're absolutely convinced of, even before they see it.

translation: And if We had willed, We could have given every soul its guidance, but the word from Me will come into effect [that] "I will surely fill Hell with jinn and people all together
commentary:
وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَـَٔاتَيْنَا كُلَّ نَفْسٍ هُدَىٰهَا وَلَـٰكِنْ حَقَّ ٱلْقَوْلُ مِنِّى لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ

And if We had willed, We would have given every soul its guidance. But the word from Me has come to pass: "I will surely fill Hell with jinn and people altogether."

Allah ﷻ addresses their desperate plea for a second chance with a profound truth: وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَـَٔاتَيْنَا كُلَّ نَفْسٍ هُدَىٰهَا - And if that's how We wanted to operate, if We had willed it, We would have given every person their guidance, every soul its particular guidance.

In other words: everybody would have been guided if Allah ﷻ wanted to force it. He could have programmed guidance into every human being, made it automatic, removed all choice. But that wasn't the point of your creation.

You were made as this marvelous creation precisely to see whether you would make the right choice on your own. Why do you think you're honored so much? Birds declare Allah's perfection - they don't have a choice. Every rock in existence glorifies Allah ﷻ - it doesn't have a choice. Angels declare Allah's perfection constantly - لَا يَعْصُونَ ٱللَّهَ مَآ أَمَرَهُمْ (66:6) - they don't have the ability to disobey Allah ﷻ in anything He commands them. They just do it.

You were supposed to be this awesome display of Allah's creative power because He gave you the choice to disobey, and you still chose to obey. That's what makes you different. That's why you're called the best of creation. And now you want to be like everybody else? Like creatures with no choice?

وَلَـٰكِنْ حَقَّ ٱلْقَوْلُ مِنِّى - However, the word from Me has come to pass, the declaration I made has become truth. What declaration? لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ ٱلْجِنَّةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ - "I will absolutely fill Hellfire with jinn and human beings altogether."

Notice who's mentioned first: jinn are mentioned before humans. Why? Because most human beings, instead of following the unseen guidance that comes from angels - which is revelation - they followed the unseen influence that came from the jinn. The whispers of Shaytan, the waswasah, the evil promptings. They followed the wrong unseen. They listened to the wrong amr. And that's what led them astray.

This isn't because Allah ﷻ programmed them to fail. He gave them everything they needed to know Him. When the angel blew the ruh into them, that fitrah was there - that natural inclination toward guidance. But they made their choices, and most people chose poorly.

translation: So taste [punishment] because you forgot the meeting of this, your Day; indeed, We have [accordingly] forgotten you. And taste the punishment of eternity for what you used to do
commentary:
فَذُوقُوا۟ بِمَا نَسِيتُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَآ إِنَّا نَسِينَـٰكُمْ ۖ وَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْخُلْدِ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ

So taste because you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours. Indeed, We have forgotten you. And taste the punishment of eternity for what you used to do.

فَذُوقُوا۟ - So taste! This is the command of consequences. بِمَا نَسِيتُمْ لِقَآءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَـٰذَآ - because you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours.

But what does "forgot" really mean here? نِسْيَان - forgetting - has two meanings in Arabic. The first is the simple inability to remember: "I can't recall your name, I don't remember who you are." But the second meaning is to dismiss someone, to deliberately ignore them, to treat them as insignificant.

Like when someone suggests, "Let's call Zayd to join us for dinner," and you say, "Nah, forget him." You're not saying you can't remember him - you're saying dismiss him, don't bother with him, he doesn't matter. Or when someone asks, "Should we wait for him?" and you respond, "Forget it, let's go" - you're choosing to disregard, to leave behind.

Allah ﷻ is saying: You "forgot" Me. You dismissed Me. You treated Me as insignificant. You undermined Me. You knew about the Day of Meeting, but you pushed it aside, treated it as unimportant, lived as if it didn't matter.

إِنَّا نَسِينَـٰكُمْ - Today We have "forgotten" you. We're dismissing you. We're treating you as you treated Us. You undermined Us, so today We're undermining you.

وَذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْخُلْدِ  - Then taste the punishment of eternity. خُلْد means permanence, everlastingness. This isn't temporary. This isn't a phase you'll get through. بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ - on account of what you used to be up to, because of what you used to do.

translation: Only those believe in Our verses who, when they are reminded by them, fall down in prostration and exalt [Allah] with praise of their Lord, and they are not arrogant
commentary:
إِنَّمَا يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهَا خَرُّوا۟ سُجَّدًا وَسَبَّحُوا۟ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ۩

Truly, the only ones who believe in Our signs are those who, when they are reminded of them, fall down in prostration and glorify the praise of their Master, and they do not show arrogance.

After describing those who rejected, Allah ﷻ now paints the opposite picture. إِنَّمَا يُؤْمِنُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا - Truly, truly, the only ones who really believe in Our miraculous signs, Our revelations, Our ayaat...

ٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِهَا - are those who, whenever they are powerfully reminded of them, when someone recites these ayaat to them, when they hear the word of Allah ﷻ...

خَرُّوا۟ سُجَّدًا - they fall down in sajdah. The word خَرَّ implies falling suddenly, collapsing from emotion. They don't just calmly bow down - they fall into prostration, overwhelmed by what they've heard.

Remember those criminals holding their heads down in shame on Judgment Day? Here are the believers putting their heads down in this life - but theirs is an act of worship, of humility before Allah ﷻ, of recognition of His greatness.

وَسَبَّحُوا۟ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ - and they declared the perfection of their Master by doing hamd, by expressing His praise, by acknowledging His favors with gratitude. Their prostration isn't just physical - it's accompanied by words that recognize Allah's perfection and thank Him.

وَهُمْ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ - and they don't show arrogance, they don't seek to make themselves great. They're not too proud to bow down. They don't think they're above this. Arrogance - kibr - is what prevented Iblis from prostrating to Adam (AS). It's what prevents people from submitting to Allah ﷻ. But these believers? They have no trace of that. They humble themselves completely.

This is an ayah of sajdah - when we recite or hear it, we perform prostration. It's our chance to embody what this ayah describes, to be among those who fall into sajdah when reminded of Allah's signs.

translation: They arise from [their] beds; they supplicate their Lord in fear and aspiration, and from what We have provided them, they spend
commentary:
تَتَجَافَىٰ جُنُوبُهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْمَضَاجِعِ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ

Their sides forsake their beds, calling upon their Master in fear and hope, and from what We have provided them, they spend.

تَتَجَافَىٰ جُنُوبُهُمْ عَنِ ٱلْمَضَاجِعِ - Their sides are restless, forsaking the beds. This needs some unpacking.

تَجَافَى comes from جَفَا, which means to not find rest in one place, to be unable to settle. When you're tossing and turning in bed, unable to sleep, uncomfortable, restless - that's تَجَافٍ. You can't find peace in that position, so you shift. Then you can't find peace in the next position, so you shift again.

Picture this scene: Your son lives in another country, and you just got news that he's in the hospital. You're calling constantly, trying to get updates. Someone finally tells you he's stable, they'll call you in the morning with more information. It's late at night. Everyone tells you, "Mom, just get some sleep." But your child is in the hospital. You lie down on one side - can't rest. You turn to the other side - still can't rest. Your mind is racing. "Can I call now? No, everyone's sleeping. They won't pick up." You're filled with two overwhelming emotions: fear for your child's wellbeing, and hope that he'll be okay.

Allah ﷻ says: This is the scene of someone who truly believes in the Akhirah. When their level of iman reaches a certain height, they feel that kind of restlessness at night. They get the feeling that the Akhirah is just around the corner, that Judgment Day is approaching. They're so aware of it, so conscious of it, that they can't simply rest peacefully in their beds.

يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا - They call upon their Master in fear and in hope. خَوْف - fear of His punishment, fear of falling short, fear of not being worthy. طَمَع - hope in His mercy, hope for His acceptance, hope for His pleasure. They're making dua to Allah ﷻ filled with both emotions simultaneously.

This is similar to what we learned in Surah Al-Furqan about the 'ibaad ar-Rahman, the servants of the Most Merciful: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمْ سُجَّدًا وَقِيَـٰمًا (25:64) - "And those who spend the night before their Master, prostrating and standing." And they say: رَبَّنَا ٱصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ (25:65) - "Our Master, avert from us the punishment of Hell!"

And here we are - if we get up for Fajr before 8am, we feel like we deserve an Olympic gold medal. If we pray Fajr within its actual time, we think we've accomplished something major. How do we bridge this gap? How do we reach these spiritual heights?

The answer: start with small, consistent steps. Everything begins with little things. You don't become spiritually transformed overnight. We need to instill healthy practices into our lives that allow us to build a relationship with Allah ﷻ.

First: Do no harm. Don't harm others, and don't harm yourself. Don't kill yourself spiritually. Don't watch filth. Don't use foul language. Don't keep company that uses bad language. Don't backbite. Every time you harm yourself or others, your heart gets damaged, more stained, more distant from Allah ﷻ.

Second: Make small, consistent adhkar. Everyone should have a copy of Hisnul Muslim, the Fortress of the Muslim. Memorize as many duas from it as possible and actually use them - when entering the house, leaving the house, going to sleep, waking up, putting clothes on, taking them off, getting in the car, reaching home, going to work, receiving a paycheck, eating food, finishing food, opening the fast, beginning the fast, entering the masjid, leaving the masjid.

Every action of yours has a beautiful dua attached to it. You don't have to memorize all of them in one day. Start with one - like the dua for leaving the house. Make sure you make it every time you leave. I'm telling you, I'm guaranteeing you: if you can instill five or six of these duas into your daily life, associated with actions you already do anyway, you will feel a spirituality you've never felt before. You'll feel closer to Allah ﷻ than you ever have.

But you have to be conscious that you're actually talking to Allah ﷻ when you make these duas. Not just that they become a subconscious habit, but that you're aware: I'm speaking to my Master right now. If you do this, you'll notice a significant difference in how you feel about Allah ﷻ and how you remember Him.

If you don't do this, you'll find it very hard to think about Allah ﷻ, to converse with Him, because God remains too abstract a concept. These simple duas that the Prophet ﷺ taught us - they're an easy way to get started. They're a real head start in developing spirituality.

My recommendation: memorize the ayaat that contain duas. Make a project out of collecting all the supplications in the Quran. Memorize them. Make dua with them. It will transform your relationship with Allah ﷻ.

The people described in this ayah reached this high state where they could make constant dua. They got so used to making dua in their daily actions that at nighttime, when hours pass without formal communication with Allah ﷻ, they can't rest. They have to come out of bed and start making dua to Allah ﷻ again.

وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ - and from what We have provided them, they spend. Their spirituality doesn't remain abstract - it manifests in action. They give from what Allah ﷻ has given them, recognizing that everything they have is from Him.

translation: And no soul knows what has been hidden for them of comfort for eyes as reward for what they used to do
commentary:
فَلَا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ مَّآ أُخْفِىَ لَهُم مِّن قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ

No soul knows what has been hidden for them of comfort to the eyes as a reward for what they used to do.

فَلَا تَعْلَمُ نَفْسٌ - No person knows, no soul has any idea. مَّآ أُخْفِىَ لَهُم - what I have kept hidden for them, what has been concealed for them.

مِّن قُرَّةِ أَعْيُنٍ - of pleasures for the eyes to see, of comfort and joy that will overwhelm the vision. قُرَّة العَيْن - literally means "coolness of the eye," something so pleasing to look at that your eyes find rest and comfort in it, something so beautiful you can't look away.

This refers primarily to the Paradise of Allah ﷻ. You've gotten some clues here and there from descriptions in the Quran and Hadith, but when you actually see it, you'll be completely taken back. Your eyes won't be able to come off of it. You'll be looking at it and crying from pure joy.

But this also refers to the good things Allah ﷻ has in store for you in this life too. No person knows what great days lie ahead for them in this world, what beautiful moments are coming, what joys are waiting. Just like the previous ayah scared us - you don't know when death is coming, where you're going to die - this ayah comforts us: you also don't know what amazing gifts Allah ﷻ has planned for you.

جَزَآءًۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ - as a reward, a compensation for what they used to do.

What action was mentioned in the ayah before? What were they doing, and at what time? They were making dua in the middle of the night. Their bodies wanted rest in bed - that's the khalq, the physical part of us that finds rest in sleep. But their ruh, their soul, said "No, I'm not at rest here. I need to get up and talk to Allah ﷻ. I need to make dua." 

That beautiful conflict inside of us - the body wanting to rest, the soul wanting to connect with Allah ﷻ. And when the soul wins, when they let their ruh be stronger than their physical desires, Allah ﷻ says: on account of what they did, on account of that choice, they have rewards beyond imagination waiting for them.

translation: Then is one who was a believer like one who was defiantly disobedient? They are not equal
commentary:
أَفَمَن كَانَ مُؤْمِنًا كَمَن كَانَ فَاسِقًا ۚ لَّا يَسْتَوُۥنَ

Is then one who is a believer like one who is corrupt? They are not equal at all.

أَفَمَن كَانَ مُؤْمِنًا - Is the one who has iman, who believes... كَمَن كَانَ فَاسِقًا - like someone who's fasiq, who's corrupt, who's transgressed, who's gone outside the bounds?

لَّا يَسْتَوُۥنَ - They are not the same at all. They're not equal in any way whatsoever. You can't compare them. They're in completely different categories.

This ayah addresses a sentiment that existed among the Quraysh. They used to say about the Prophet ﷺ and the other Muslims: "These people aren't special. Look at us - we're from noble families. We're intelligent, well-spoken, respected leaders. What makes them different? What's the big deal about them? We don't see such a huge difference. Why can't we be considered just as good, or even better?"

Allah ﷻ is saying: Can you even compare a believer with a fasiq? Are they remotely the same? The answer is absolutely not - not in any dimension, not in this life or the next, not in their inner reality or their ultimate destination. What then to speak of comparing the Messenger of Allah ﷺ himself with the disbelievers? There's no comparison whatsoever.

translation: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, for them will be the Gardens of Refuge as accommodation for what they used to do
commentary:
أَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَلَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتُ ٱلْمَأْوَىٰ نُزُلًۢا بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ

As for those who believed and acted righteously, for them are the Gardens of Refuge as hospitality for what they used to do.

أَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ - As for those who have iman and have acted with righteousness, who combined belief with righteous action...

فَلَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتُ ٱلْمَأْوَىٰ - for them are the Gardens of Refuge, the Gardens of Shelter.

نُزُلًۢا - as initial hospitality, as a welcoming gift. This word نُزُل is beautiful. In Arabic, when someone comes down from a journey - they come down from their horse, their camel, they come down from a plane, they arrive at your door - at that moment of arrival, they're called نَازِل, and what you give them right away is called نُزُل.

It's like when someone arrives from the airport. You pick them up, and in the car you give them a bottle of water, maybe some snacks - that's nuzul, initial hospitality. You get them home and say, "Why don't you rest in that room? Get some sleep, freshen up." That's still nuzul. Later, when they wake up, you serve them the full meal, the complete feast - that's not nuzul anymore, that's called مُقَام, the fuller hospitality.

Allah ﷻ is saying: in Jannah, you'll be brought into this magnificent palace where you're told to relax and rest. You're given this incredible initial welcome, and Allah ﷻ calls it nuzul. As if to say: "We're just getting started. This is just the water bottle and snacks in the car. Your real party begins after this."

My favorite ayah about nuzul is in Surah Al-Kahf. Allah ﷻ says about the believers: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتُ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسِ نُزُلًا (18:107) - "Indeed, those who believed and acted righteously, for them are the Gardens of Firdaws as nuzul."

Firdaws is described as the highest level of Paradise, and Allah ﷻ calls it their nuzul - their initial welcome! So what's beyond that? What comes after the highest level of Paradise is just your initial hospitality? SubhanAllah - it's beyond our imagination.

بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ - on account of what they used to do, as a reward for their actions.

translation: But as for those who defiantly disobeyed, their refuge is the Fire. Every time they wish to emerge from it, they will be returned to it while it is said to them, "Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny
commentary:
وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا۟ فَمَأْوَىٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ ۖ كُلَّمَآ أَرَادُوٓا۟ أَن يَخْرُجُوا۟ مِنْهَآ أُعِيدُوا۟ فِيهَا وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ

And as for those who transgressed, their refuge is the Fire. Every time they want to come out of it, they are returned into it, and it is said to them: "Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to deny!"

وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ فَسَقُوا۟ - And as for those who were corrupt, who transgressed the bounds, who went outside the limits Allah ﷻ set...

فَمَأْوَىٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ - their place of refuge, their shelter, their dwelling is the Fire. Notice the word مَأْوَى is used for both Paradise and Hell - both are described as places of refuge, places where you'll be staying. But what a difference between the two!

كُلَّمَآ أَرَادُوٓا۟ أَن يَخْرُجُوا۟ مِنْهَآ - Every single time they want, they desire, they intend to come out of it...

Notice Allah ﷻ doesn't even say "every time they try to get out." They can't even try. They can't even attempt it. All they can do is want. Even the attempt is taken away from them. Just the desire is there, and that's it.

أُعِيدُوا۟ فِيهَا - they are returned into it, pushed back into it. Every time the want arises, it's as though each time they feel that desire, they're taken even deeper inside.

وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ - and it is said to them: ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ - "Taste the punishment of the Fire which you used to lie about, which you used to deny, which you used to call false!"

translation: And we will surely let them taste the nearer punishment short of the greater punishment that perhaps they will repent
commentary:
وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْأَدْنَىٰ دُونَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْأَكْبَرِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ

And We will surely make them taste the lesser punishment before the greater punishment, so that perhaps they may return.

وَلَنُذِيقَنَّهُم - And We will absolutely, certainly make them taste... مِّنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْأَدْنَىٰ - some of the nearer punishment, the closer punishment, the lesser punishment.

دُونَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ٱلْأَكْبَرِ - before, way before, in front of the greatest punishment. In other words, there's the big punishment - the punishment of the Hereafter - but before that comes, Allah ﷻ gives smaller punishments in this life.

This is a critical ayah because it continues the theme from the previous surah. Allah ﷻ gives nations that He sends messengers to small warnings, smaller tests, lesser difficulties - so they can wake up and get their act together before the major punishment arrives.

Fir'awn was given nine signs, and seven of them were difficulties, challenges to that community. The Prophet ﷺ and the Muslims were given Badr, they were given various tests and trials. This is all what? Lesser punishment before the greater punishment arrives.

The smaller punishment is supposed to be a wake-up call, a teaser for what's coming if you don't change. You get a taste so you understand: "I better get my act together." But if you don't respond, then the greater punishment is on its way.

لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ - so that perhaps, hopefully, they may return. Return to what? To Allah ﷻ, to guidance, to truth, to righteousness. The purpose of the lesser punishment isn't cruelty - it's mercy. It's a chance to turn back before it's too late.

translation: And who is more unjust than one who is reminded of the verses of his Lord; then he turns away from them? Indeed We, from the criminals, will take retribution
commentary:
وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّن ذُكِّرَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ ثُمَّ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهَآ ۚ إِنَّا مِنَ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ مُنتَقِمُونَ

And who is more wrong than one who is reminded of the signs of his Master, then he turns away from them? Indeed, We will take revenge on the criminals.

وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ - And who could be a worse wrongdoer, who could be more oppressive, more unjust... مِمَّن ذُكِّرَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ - than someone who was reminded of the ayaat of his Master, the signs of his Lord?

Right now, I'm reminding myself and you of the ayaat of Allah ﷻ

translation: And We certainly gave Moses the Scripture, so do not be in doubt over his meeting. And we made the Torah guidance for the Children of Israel
And We made from among them leaders guiding by Our command when they were patient and [when] they were certain of Our signs
Indeed, your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that over which they used to differ
Has it not become clear to them how many generations We destroyed before them, [as] they walk among their dwellings? Indeed in that are signs; then do they not hear
Have they not seen that We drive the water [in clouds] to barren land and bring forth thereby crops from which their livestock eat and [they] themselves? Then do they not see
And they say, "When will be this conquest, if you should be truthful
Say, [O Muhammad], "On the Day of Conquest the belief of those who had disbelieved will not benefit them, nor will they be reprieved
So turn away from them and wait. Indeed, they are waiting
